baseline.notes: Run 36 introduced the GK-proximity gate but co-visibility was the bottleneck: of 73 GK events, only ~5 had ball+GK in the same sampled frame. 46% shot_stop_diving events had no ball detection, 40% had no GK detection. Gate cut 4 FPs (zero TP loss) — directionally right but way under target.

targets.notes: Co-visibility target ≥25% (up from ~7%). If that lifts, the proximity gate can actually do its job. F1 ≥ 0.42 is the breakthrough target; beating Run 32 (0.396) is the minimum recovery.

notes: Run #36b — GK-specific sampling. Same GK-proximity rule as Run #36 but: n_frames 5→10, min_span 2s→6s, inference_size 640→1280 for GK events. Non-GK events unchanged. Expected to lift GK ball+GK co-visibility from ~7% to ≥25%, enabling the proximity gate to actually make decisions rather than fail-open.
